title: Managers and proprietors in forestry, fishing and related services
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the management or operation of farms, ranches, greenhouses, aquacultural operations, nurseries, timber tracts, or other agricultural establishments. May hire, train, and supervise farm workers or contract for services to carry out the day-to-day activities of the managed operation. May engage in or supervise planting, cultivating, harvesting, and financial and marketing activities. Feed, water, and examine pets and other nonfarm animals for signs of illness, disease, or injury in laboratories and animal hospitals and clinics. Clean and disinfect cages and work areas, and sterilize laboratory and surgical equipment. May provide routine postoperative care, administer medication orally or topically, or prepare samples for laboratory examination under the supervision of veterinary or laboratory animal technologists or technicians, veterinarians, or scientists. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of entertainment and recreation related workers. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Provide information to customers on the care of trees, shrubs, flowers, plants, and lawns.
Direct and monitor the transfer of mature fish to lakes, ponds, streams, or commercial tanks, and oversee trapping and spawning operations.
Monitor environments to ensure maintenance of optimum animal or plant life and to detect disease or unhealthy conditions requiring medical care.
Monitor activities such as irrigation, chemical application, harvesting, and breeding to ensure adherence to safety regulations or standards.
Hire, supervise, and train support workers, and oversee the scheduling of work crews and equipment for various operations.
Evaluate marketing or sales alternatives for products and analyze market conditions to determine acreage allocations.
Direct crop production operations such as planning, tilling, planting, fertilizing, and harvesting to optimize yield.
Determine types or quantities of crops, plants, or livestock to be grown based on budgets, market conditions, and central government incentives.
Conduct inspections to determine crop maturity or condition and supervise stock examinations to identify disease or parasites.
Administer prescribed medications to animals, treat minor injuries, and contact veterinarians for serious illnesses.
Feed and water animals, clean and disinfect pens and cages, and perform general animal care tasks.
Collect and record growth, production, environmental, and animal data related to care and breeding practices.
Maintain financial, operational, and production records for farms or aquaculture operations and perform accounting duties.
Inspect facilities and equipment for signs of disrepair, and perform necessary maintenance work.
Coordinate clerical, record-keeping, inventory, requisitioning, and marketing activities related to farm or fishery operations.
Negotiate with buyers for the sale, storage, or shipment of crops or livestock while directing the breeding of stock.
Oversee the purchase of supplies, gear, and equipment necessary for farming and fishing operations.
Educate or advise clients on animal health care, nutrition, or behavior problems related to pets or livestock.
Perform both supervisory and management functions, including overseeing hiring, marketing, and operational standards.
Transport or arrange for transport of animals, equipment, food, and other supplies to and from work sites.
Inspect buildings, fences, fields, supplies, and equipment to determine work to be performed.
Read inventory records, customer orders, or shipping schedules to determine required activities.
Calculate or monitor budgets for maintenance or development of collections, grounds, or infrastructure.
Confer with managers to determine production requirements, equipment conditions, or work schedules.
Requisition or purchase necessary supplies, such as insecticides, machine parts, or tools.
Monitor operations to identify and solve problems, and ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Plan work schedules according to personnel and equipment availability and ensure timely delivery of service.
Inspect crops, fields, or plant stock to determine conditions and need for cultivating or harvesting.
Assign tasks such as feeding and treatment of animals, cleaning, and maintenance of animal quarters.
Observe animals for signs of illness, injury, or unusual behavior, notifying veterinarians as warranted.
Investigate employee complaints and resolve problems following management rules and regulations.
Train workers in techniques such as planting, harvesting, and use of equipment, as well as safety measures.
Direct or coordinate activities of workers, ensuring quality service and compliance with specifications.
Meet with managers to stay informed of changes affecting operations or worker performance.
